units.verbatim_text: I feel like Zaya is like my 40th birthday present. I turned 40 recently, and shortly before I turned 40, it was Susu's birthday in February. So it was like God has done a marvelous thing in our lives, and we're so grateful and we're filled with celebration and joy. He's not letting us sleep, but we're enjoying every sleepless minute of it. Are you good? You all right? I think this is a good time for me to reintroduce my queen. Let me take my time with it. Baby, don't stand up here. Don't stand up yet.
units.context: [Banky expresses gratitude for his son and introduces his wife, highlighting family joy and blessings.]

units.verbatim_text: The first key to having final say faith is relationship. Do we have a relationship with God? Amen. Are you guys here this morning? I know the service has taken a little bit longer. We can share the grace and go, or we can get into this world. Amen. All right, let's do it. And so the point in relationship is it's not enough to know that God is real. It's not enough to even know that Jesus died and resurrected because we know that. But God, you have to move past the point like to be loved.
units.context: [Banky emphasizes the importance of having a personal relationship with God as the foundation of faith.]

units.verbatim_text: What is God's love language? You ever thought about that? What language do you speak to God to let him know that you love him? And that what's God's love language? One of his love languages is faith. The Bible says without faith it is impossible. So you can be doing everything, but if you don't trust him, and so sometimes God will take you down this journey to say I need to get you to this place where you trust me completely, absolutely, not because of what I'm doing or not doing or my timeline or your timeline.
units.context: [Banky discusses faith as God's love language and the necessity of trusting God completely.]

units.verbatim_text: Having faith in God will not stop you from crying. Having faith in God will not stop bad times from coming. It won't stop you from hurting, but your faith in God will help you heal. And it'll help you get back up, and it'll help you keep going, and it will help you get the answer that you're looking for. Because sometimes you can be in a relationship with God and things die, dreams die, prayers die, things that you have been waiting on, they just die.
units.context: [Banky acknowledges that faith does not prevent pain but helps in healing and perseverance.]

units.verbatim_text: Point number two is worship. And how many people know that it can be hard to worship when you're going through warfare? It can be hard to praise when you are in pain, and that's why the Bible calls it a sacrifice of praise. Sometimes praise is a sacrifice. Are you with me? In fact, I mean, are you with us? Sometimes praise is a sacrifice. If we had time, I'd take you to Acts chapter 16 when it says Paul and Silas not only are they minding their business, they're actually minding God's business.
units.context: [Banky highlights worship as a key element of faith, especially during difficult times.]

units.verbatim_text: Giving is an act of worship. Giving is an act of love. And me, I come with some radical idea. You know me, I'm crazy, but I always like to use because it's no these are tough truths. And I use Susu is like my she's my sounding board. She's, you know, she's who I go to for advice before I try to do anything. So when I felt God saying that I came to so I said, you know, this car that we used to use to do I think that God wants us to sell it and donate the money to the church.
units.context: [Banky discusses giving as an act of worship and shares a personal story of sacrificial giving.]

units.verbatim_text: We had decided that we were going to stay at least a year without doing anything. We felt like that's what God was telling us. Like we literally felt like God was saying stop, just stop doing everything. Don't no test, no pill, no nothing. There's nothing wrong with you. Stop. So we had said that you know what this is what God is saying to us just go back to the place of trust. Then we went to Pastor Tony. Then we went to Pastor Tony we're having like a one of our morning meetings where we go for prayer meetings.
units.context: [Banky and Adesua decide to pause medical interventions, trusting in God's plan and timing.]

units.verbatim_text: Why did Jesus, what did the Bible say, so when he heard Lazarus was sick, he waited two more days. In total, four days passed before Jesus showed up. So why did he do that? Why would Jesus love us and not come when we want? It is because you have to get to the place where you accept that God's plan is better and God has a bigger picture in mind. Do you know that Lazarus' death was the tipping point that led Jesus to the cross? If you keep reading in the chapter, it's after he raised this.
units.context: [Banky explains the importance of trusting in God's timing and the bigger picture of His plans.]
